text: Multimodal visual information fusion aims to combine multi-sensor data into a fused image which contains more complementary and less redundant information. However, the complementary information is difficult to extract, especially in the case of highly similar infrared and visible images. The common cross attention modules only consider the correlation of features, while image fusion tasks are actually focusing on the complementarity (uncorrelation). Therefore, a novel cross attention mechanism (CAM) is proposed to enhance complementary information in this paper. A two-stage training strategy based fusion scheme is introduced to obtain the fused images. In the first stage, two auto-encoder networks with the same architecture are trained for each modality. In the second stage, with the fixed encoders, the CAM and a decoder are trained. With the trained CAM, features extracted from two modalities are integrated into one fused feature in which the complementary information is enhanced and the redundant features are reduced. Finally, the fused image can be generated by the trained decoder. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed fusion method obtains the SOTA fusion performance compared with the existing fusion networks. Our codes will be available soon.
explanation: Contains polished academic language and describes a method with generic performance claims but no empirical results or citations; this style is common in AI-generated summaries, though a human-authored abstract could also resemble it.
